rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Platform claims in body (auto-load tiers, @import wiring) match compendium §6d.
No shortcut or path claims that contradict §6.
Body correctly names the three auto-load filenames (./CLAUDE.local.md, ./CLAUDE.md, ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md) and the @import wiring path for arbitrary files.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Sessions are the unit of work
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Body uses 'session' language correctly (the M2 plan-mode session, this session).
No external practitioner citations or load-bearing claims in body — exercise grounds in the student's own session evidence.
No statistics cited.

Four fenced blocks; no [BRACKET] placeholders. Final optional prompt uses open-hook pattern (Ticket: at end of fence) — correct shape for student-supplied input where the student has the ticket more concretely than Claude.
Phase 1/2/3 each carry a clear lead-in. The optional ticket prompt has body-prose lead-in ('Then ask Claude to infer how the fields are being used').
All prompts use **Prompt** *(Claude Code)* label exactly; no custom labels; no copy-prefix or end-of-prompt closers.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Skills by name, never by path
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No skill invocations in this exercise.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Chain prompts by back-reference and deterministic path
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Phase 1 chains on scrollback. Phase 2 chains on Phase 1 output by reference. Phase 3 disambiguates 'this rules file' as 'the .md file you saved in Phase 2' in body.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No markdown shout inside fenced prompts
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No **bold**/*italic* inside fences.
Prompts have 2-3 instructions each — under the 4+ sub-step trigger; no plan-mode invocation that would require a structural preamble suppressor.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No CWD restatement inside prompt body
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No absolute paths or cd commands inside fences. Phase 2 deliberately leaves location to student choice.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Name the consumer at write time
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Phase 2 names the consumer through the loading-model framing — Claude reads the file on future sessions when placed in an auto-load surface or wired via @import. The file is not a (c)-only chat-substitute write.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No module-name references in prompt prose
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: All four fences are M-reference-free in prose. Body 'Phase 1/2/3' is exercise-scoped phase labels, not module numbers. Maintainer-block M-references are exempt.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Disambiguation and decision logic inside the prompt fence
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Phase 1 fence carries the rewrite-or-reject pivot inline. Phase 2 fence carries the path-taxonomy ask + confirm-wording loop + overwrite-check inline. Body adds @import wiring as student context, but each prompt fence carries its own runtime logic.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Forcing functions live in prompts, not body
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Each phase's forcing function (anchor-each-rule + rewrite/reject, two-three-paths + show-back-first-three, name-shapes + no-code) lives inside the fence.
Exercise produces a rules.md file; no verification machinery is built that would need a smoke-test step.

Phase 2 body surfaces the auto-load tier (fires-anywhere vs fires-only-when-repo-open) and the @import wiring bridging explicit-load files into auto-load. Tier mechanics are reified through path choice, not lectured.

platform_claims_checked.claim: fires-anywhere-on-this-laptop (user-level) vs fires-only-when-this-repo-is-open (repo-personal) distinguish auto-load tiers
adding @~/.claude/memory/task-shaping.md to ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md wires a non-auto-load file into auto-load
